name: jvm-extract
description: Extract J9 JVM runtime, UI framework JARs, and application config for offline reverse engineering — the first step toward custom MMI menu entries
author: dspl1236
scripts: jvm_extract.sh - Copy JVM, JARs, configs, and run string analysis on lsd.jxe
extracts: lsd/lsd.jxe - Main UI application (~25MB J9 JXE blob)
lsd/AppDevelopment.jar - GEM plugin (decompile to understand bundle architecture)
lsd/lsd.sh - JVM startup script (classpath, JVM flags, system properties)
lsd/*.jar - All JARs (DSITracer, texts, myAudi connect, etc.)
j9/ - J9 JVM binary and java.properties
jxe-analysis/ - Extracted strings: bundle config, class names, CAN refs, menu structure
notes: Read-only operation — copies files to SD card, changes nothing on the MMI. Output is ~30-50MB depending on firmware. The jxe-analysis folder contains pre-extracted strings from lsd.jxe highlighting bundle registration, Java class names, CAN/vehicle data references, and menu/screen structure — no decompiler needed for initial reconnaissance.
